title: The five pillars of Islam
reference: Hadith of Jibril — Sahih al-Bukhari & Sahih Muslim (Kitab al-Iman)
content: Islam is built on five pillars: the testimony of faith (Shahadah) that there is no god but Allah and Muhammad is His Messenger, establishing the five daily prayers (Salah), giving Zakat, fasting in Ramadan, and Hajj to the House for whoever is able.

title: Obligation of Salah and its fixed times
content: Indeed, the prayer has been decreed upon the believers at specified times. The five daily prayers are obligatory upon every sane, adult Muslim and must be offered within their appointed times.

title: Obligation of Zakat
reference: Qur'an, Sura al-Baqarah 2:43 and Sura at-Tawbah 9:103
content: Establish prayer and give Zakat. Zakat is obligatory on a free, sane, adult Muslim who owns wealth reaching the nisab (the minimum threshold) and on which a full lunar year has passed. The standard rate on monetary wealth is one fortieth (2.5%).

title: Fasting in Ramadan
content: Fasting (Sawm) in the month of Ramadan is obligatory upon every sane, adult Muslim who is able. O you who believe, fasting is prescribed for you as it was prescribed for those before you, that you may attain taqwa.

title: Wudu (ablution) commanded for prayer
content: When you rise to pray, wash your faces and your forearms to the elbows, wipe your heads, and wash your feet to the ankles. Wudu is a condition for the validity of Salah.

title: Reciting Surah al-Fatiha in prayer
reference: Sahih al-Bukhari & Sahih Muslim — 'There is no prayer for one who does not recite the Opening of the Book'
content: Recitation of Surah al-Fatiha is established in Salah. In the Hanafi school, behind the imam the follower (muqtadi) does not recite; the imam's recitation suffices for those praying behind him.
